big_picture: Indian airlines plan to operate 58 evacuation flights to West Asia on Wednesday, the government said. The U.S. Supreme Court struck down the tariffs imposed by Donald Trump, raising questions about reciprocal tariffs. The escalating Middle East conflict has fueled optimism for Indian defence firms, with expectations of increased export orders. The Jammu & Kashmir High Court ruled that couples seeking protection must file an affidavit stating whether it is their first marriage. The Indian government is considering measures to ensure the safety of the diaspora and provide a fuel price cushion for the middle class amid the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict.

articles.title: Persons Not Liable for Registration: A Comprehensive Analysis
articles.summary: The article explains that certain individuals, such as those making only exempt or non-taxable supplies and agriculturists, are not required to register for GST under Section 23 of the CGST Act. articles.raw_summary: Section 23 of the CGST Act specifies categories of persons who are not liable to obtain GST registration regardless of turnover. The provision exempts persons making only exempt or non-taxable supplies and agriculturists supplying produce from cultivation. articles.title: MNRE Sets Emission Thresholds for Green Ammonia and Green Methanol
articles.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has set emission thresholds and certification frameworks for green ammonia and green methanol as part of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. This move aims to promote the production and use of green fuels, aligning with the government's broader climate and energy goals. The mission, approved in 2023 with a ₹197.44 billion budget, seeks to boost demand, production, and exports of green hydrogen and related fuels.
articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has notified standards for green ammonia and green methanol under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, establishing emission thresholds and certification frameworks for both fuels. The Union Cabinet had approved the National Green Hydrogen Mission in 2023 to facilitate demand creation, production, utilization, and export of green hydrogen with an initial outlay of ₹197.44 billion (~$2.16 billion).

articles.title: Enforcement, yes — but with accountability: Understanding arrest provisions in GST law

Former CBIC chairman Najib Shah highlights that legal grounds alone do not mandate arrests, ensuring a balanced approach to enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Given the large-scale detection of tax evasion cases, which have a multiplier effect on the economy and may even fund anti-national activities, arrest provisions are necessary in fiscal laws. However, the Board’s circular makes it clear that the mere existence of legal grounds for arrest does not automatically mandate an arrest, writes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms
articles.summary: The Indian government has revised the BIS rules to speed up product certification and tighten compliance norms. This change places more emphasis on documentation scrutiny and third-party laboratory testing. The move aims to support manufacturing expansion in the country.
articles.raw_summary: Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion.

articles.summary: The Indian government collected ₹1.61 trillion in GST revenue in February, following a rate cut announced in September last year. The government hopes this will boost demand and support higher tax collections. This is a significant government action in the finance sector.
articles.raw_summary: The government has maintained that GST rate cuts announced in September last year would boost demand for goods and services and, over time, support higher tax collections.

The government is closely monitoring the situation.

articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India has awarded a major project to build the Bhubaneswar Bypass. This 111 km, six-lane ring road will connect Rameshwar to Tangi. articles.title: Qatar declares force majeure, gas supplies to Indian industry cut amid Iranian strikes
articles.summary: India faces significant energy disruption as Qatar halts liquefied natural gas deliveries. An Iranian drone strike has forced Qatar to declare force majeure. This halt impacts up to 40 percent of India's gas supplies. Industries are seeing cuts, while CNG and piped gas networks are prioritized. Companies are seeking alternative supplies in a firming spot market.

articles.title: India’s growth is structural, not cyclical
articles.summary: India's economy is growing through investment and reforms, not just stimulus. Public spending on infrastructure is rising, boosting competitiveness. Tax stability and fewer regulations encourage investors. Reforms are being implemented, with a focus on efficient project delivery. Labour laws are being simplified to support manufacturing. Faster dispute resolution is also key.
